cover_letter: I am excited to apply for the role at GeistM. As a senior at Indiana University?s Kelley School of Business, dual majoring in Marketing and International Business, I have combined my love for marketing strategy with a deep curiosity for how brands connect across cultures. My internships in both the U.S. and abroad, along with immersive academic experiences in South Africa and Hungary, have shaped my ability to craft creative, data-informed campaigns that resonate with diverse audiences. Most recently, as a Marketing Intern with Schmidt Brothers in New York City, I developed personas and creative assets for a Back-to-School campaign, modernized 80+ product descriptions for SEO and clarity, and produced social content to grow engagement. I also analyzed eCommerce performance across Shopify, Amazon Marketplace, and Google Analytics, translating data into strategies for the holiday season. Previously, at Impact Hub Berlin, I designed multi-channel campaigns and leveraged HubSpot CRM to segment and target audiences for increased event participation. Whether leading a cross-functional capstone team to launch a go-to-market strategy or researching generational purchasing behavior, I thrive in environments where I can merge creative problem-solving with a global perspective.
extra_context: Strong marketing student targeting NYC full-time roles with direct internship proof in content, campaigns, analytics, and eCommerce.
jobText:
About the job
Our Story

At Madewell, we believe that great style starts with great design. Since 2006, we?ve been creating premium denim and foundational wardrobe pieces for men and women that balance quality, authenticity, and versatility. From iconic jeans perfected by denim experts to the essential tee, the most versatile shoe, and the timeless leather bag?if it?s Madewell, it?s made well.

Our dedication to Diversity, Equity, Belonging & Sustainability has been celebrated with industry recognition, reflecting our commitment to fostering an inclusive and sustainable workplace.

The Role + Purpose

As Ecommerce Analytics Coordinator based in New York City you will be supporting the Digital Strategy & Analytics Manager with reporting, analysis, and strategy of Madewell?s ecommerce business. You will work cross-functionally with Marketing, CRM, Site, App, Merchandising, Planning, and Tech teams. This position reports to the Digital Strategy & Analytics Manager and is part of the Ecommerce team.

Responsibilities:

Website + app analytics

Build and manage reports for the ecommerce business reflecting performance of website and app KPIs against trends and benchmarks
Actively monitor conversion funnel metrics, identify opportunities for conversion rate optimization
Support work with technical teams for site tagging and implementation decisions

Performance marketing

Support with marketing channel forecasting and scenario planning to align KPIs with financial goals
Provide weekly, monthly and quarterly reporting to key stakeholders on marketing channel performance

Ecommerce strategy

Deliver performance recaps and insights for ecommerce events and promotions
Support ad hoc data requests and analyses from cross-functional teams as it relates to driving the ecommerce business (ie. Loyalty, CRM, Site Merchandising)

Qualifications:

0-3 years of experience in analytical role, experience in retail industry not required
Bachelor?s degree in a quantitative field (ie. Finance, Business, Economics, Statistics, Mathematics)
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint preferred
Strong analytical skills with an aptitude for problem solving
Strong written and verbal communication and attention to detail
Ability to manage multiple workstreams and remain organized
Intellectual curiosity and desire to learn
Team player with an ability to build constructive and effective relationships across a broad and diverse group of business partners
Experience in Looker, Google Analytics, PowerBI, SQL a plus but not required

metadata.expected_direction: likely review or apply; adjacent growth-marketing fit with meaningful ecommerce analytics upside but heavier reporting/quant demands than GeistM
